references.text: [Gu04] Guy, Richard K., Unsolved problems in number theory. (2004), xviii+437.

remarks.text:
Conjectured by Erd\H{o}s and Tur\'{a}n. They also suggest the stronger conjecture that $\limsup 1_A\ast 1_A(n)/\log n>0$. 

Another stronger conjecture would be that the hypothesis $\lvert A\cap [1,N]\rvert \gg N^{1/2}$ for all large $N$ suffices.

See also [40], and [1145] for a stronger generalisation.

This is discussed in problem C9 of Guy's collection \cite{Gu04}.

statement.text: If $A\subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is such that $A+A$ contains all but finitely many integers then $\limsup 1_A\ast 1_A(n)=\infty$.
